qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Documented sample connector exposes unauthenticated file-manager access
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets unauthenticated access in the sample PHP connector templates, while the advisory is a command injection in the resize command's bg parameter on ImageMagick CLI code paths. The finding does not identify the advisory's vulnerable function, parameter flow, or shell-command construction root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described resize bg parameter command injection in ImageMagick CLI handling. The only qualifying finding is a separate missing-authentication issue in the documented sample connector templates.
evidence: GHSA-8q4h-8crm-5cvc describes command injection in the resize command when the attacker-controlled bg parameter is interpolated into ImageMagick CLI shell commands without sufficient validation or escaping.

The only final qualifying finding is unauthenticated-sample-connector-access, which reports missing authentication in php/connector.minimal.php-dist and php/connector.maximal.php-dist after the documented rename-and-serve deployment step.

That finding's exploit path and PoC focus on unauthenticated open/file/upload/rm requests to the sample connector and do not involve the resize command, the bg parameter, ImageMagick CLI backend selection, or shell metacharacter injection.
